title: ISigmaSeparation
lean_code_with_comments:
def IsISigmaSeparated (G : CDMG Node) (A B C : Set Node) : Prop :=
  тИА {u v : Node} (╧А : Walk G u v),
      u тИИ A тЖТ v тИИ (G.J : Set Node) тИк B тЖТ ╧А.IsSigmaBlockedGiven C

def IsNotISigmaSeparated (G : CDMG Node) (A B C : Set Node) : Prop :=
  ┬м G.IsISigmaSeparated A B C

abbrev IsISigmaSeparatedEmpty (G : CDMG Node) (A B : Set Node) : Prop :=
  G.IsISigmaSeparated A B тИЕ

abbrev IsSigmaSeparated (G : CDMG Node) (A B C : Set Node) : Prop :=
  G.IsISigmaSeparated A B C

abbrev IsNotSigmaSeparated (G : CDMG Node) (A B C : Set Node) : Prop :=
  G.IsNotISigmaSeparated A B C
lean_code_without_comments:
def IsISigmaSeparated (G : CDMG Node) (A B C : Set Node) : Prop :=
  тИА {u v : Node} (╧А : Walk G u v),
      u тИИ A тЖТ v тИИ (G.J : Set Node) тИк B тЖТ ╧А.IsSigmaBlockedGiven C

def IsNotISigmaSeparated (G : CDMG Node) (A B C : Set Node) : Prop :=
  ┬м G.IsISigmaSeparated A B C

abbrev IsISigmaSeparatedEmpty (G : CDMG Node) (A B : Set Node) : Prop :=
  G.IsISigmaSeparated A B тИЕ

abbrev IsSigmaSeparated (G : CDMG Node) (A B C : Set Node) : Prop :=
  G.IsISigmaSeparated A B C

abbrev IsNotSigmaSeparated (G : CDMG Node) (A B C : Set Node) : Prop :=
  G.IsNotISigmaSeparated A B C
lean_explanation:
This file formalises def_3_18 (i-$\sigma$-separation) as five declarations on a CDMG `G : CDMG Node`. The polymorphic carrier `Node : Type*` with `[DecidableEq Node]` is the chapter-wide convention тАФ the ambient vertex type is left generic, with decidable equality required by the `CDMG`'s `Finset` machinery. The three subset arguments `A B C : Set Node` correspond to the LN's $A, B, C \subseteq J \cup V$; the carrier is `Set Node` rather than `Finset Node` so that empty sets and arbitrary set-algebraic combinations are admitted, matching the LN's "not necessarily non-empty" permissiveness and `def_3_17`'s `IsSigmaBlockedGiven` parameter shape.

The central predicate `IsISigmaSeparated G A B C` is a universal over walks. The walk binders `{u v : Node}` are implicit (Lean infers endpoints from the walk's type) and `(╧А : Walk G u v)` is explicit, mirroring `def_3_16`'s `IsBlockableNonCollider` shape тАФ the walk is the primary subject, not its endpoints. `Walk G u v` is the walk type of `def_3_4` item i. (a tuple of incident edges), and `╧А.IsSigmaBlockedGiven C` is dot-notation for `def_3_17`'s blocking predicate. Read aloud, the body says "for every walk $\pi$ with $v_0 \in A$ and $v_n \in J \cup B$, $\pi$ is $C$-$\sigma$-blocked", word-for-word with the LN.

The right-endpoint constraint `v тИИ (G.J : Set Node) тИк B` involves a coercion: `G.J : Finset Node` lifts to `Set Node` so the set-union with `B` is well-typed. Length-zero walks (`Walk.nil`) are in scope by construction; their $\sigma$-blocking status is determined by `def_3_17` at the length-zero case (where `IsSigmaBlockedGiven` reduces to `False`).

The remaining four declarations are unfoldings and notation aliases: `IsNotISigmaSeparated` is `┬м IsISigmaSeparated` (the LN's $\nisPerp$); `IsISigmaSeparatedEmpty` is the $C = \emptyset$ shorthand; `IsSigmaSeparated` and `IsNotSigmaSeparated` are the $J = \emptyset$ notation aliases (the LN's $\sPerp$ / $\nsPerp$). The three notational shorthands are `abbrev`s тАФ definitionally transparent, so lemmas about the underlying predicate fire on the shorthand without an explicit `unfold`. None add fresh content; each is a notational renaming kept definitionally linked to `IsISigmaSeparated`.
design_choices:
**`Set Node` rather than `Finset Node` for `A`, `B`, `C`.** The LN reads "$A, B, C \subseteq J \cup V$, not necessarily non-empty"; `Set Node` matches that permissiveness, carries the boolean algebra (`тИк`, `тИй`, `тИЕ`) needed to spell `J тИк B` and `C = тИЕ` directly, interoperates with `G.AncSet` (used inside `IsSigmaBlockedGiven`) without coercion, and matches `def_3_17`'s `C : Set Node`. `Finset Node` would force consumers to supply finiteness even when only set algebra is needed; consumers holding a `Finset` can pass `(тЖСs : Set Node)`. No `A тКЖ J тИк V` hypothesis is enforced either тАФ out-of-graph nodes contribute vacuously.

**Asymmetric inclusion of `J` on the right endpoint is deliberate.** The LN's footnote `fn:why-J` puts $J$ on the right (`v тИИ J тИк B`) rather than just `B`, so the implied (asymmetric) separoid rules for $id$/$i\sigma$-separation match those of conditional independence under Markov kernels. A direct consequence: the marginal case $B = \emptyset$ is *not* vacuous when $J \neq \emptyset$; `IsISigmaSeparated A тИЕ C` asserts every walk from $A$ into the input nodes $J$ is $C$-$\sigma$-blocked. This shape is load-bearing for the chapter 4+ Markov-property results тАФ readers tempted to symmetrise to recover the usual literature convention should not.

**Endpoint coercion via `(G.J : Set Node) тИк B`.** `G.J : Finset Node` and `B : Set Node` are different types, so the Finset-level union `G.J тИк B` is ill-typed; coercing `G.J` to `Set Node` and unioning at the set level is the only well-typed move. This lifts every `Set`-algebra lemma (`Set.mem_union`, `Set.union_empty`) directly to `IsISigmaSeparated`. The boolean spelling `v тИИ G.J тИи v тИИ B` would be definitionally equivalent but lose the "$J \cup B$ as a set" reading the LN expects.

**Walk-first universal, not membership-first.** The walk is the primary subject of `тИА {u v} (╧А : Walk G u v), тАж`; endpoint memberships are properties of its endpoints. The signature reads "for every walk $\pi$ with $v_0 \in A$ and $v_n \in J \cup B$, $\pi$ is $C$-$\sigma$-blocked", word-for-word with the LN. A membership-first variant pushes membership ahead of the walk and adds nesting at the call site.

**Negation as a definitionally-equal `def`, not a parallel existential.** `IsNotISigmaSeparated G A B C` unfolds to `┬м IsISigmaSeparated G A B C` by `rfl`, so the two predicates stay definitionally linked at every interchange site. The tex's "equivalently, $\exists$ walk тАж not $C$-$\sigma$-blocked" reformulation is the classical De Morgan dual of the negated universal тАФ derivable as a one-line lemma when needed, not a parallel definition that would require a classical bridging lemma at every interconversion.

**`abbrev` for the three notational shorthands.** LN items 3 and 4 are pure notation: $A \isPerp_G B$ is *defined to mean* $A \isPerp_G B \given \emptyset$, and $A \sPerp_G B \given C$ is the renaming under $J = \emptyset$. `abbrev` is fully transparent тАФ lemmas targeting `IsISigmaSeparated` fire on the shorthand without an `unfold`. A `def` would create an opaque alias forcing every consumer interchanging the two forms to invoke `unfold`. Crucially, no `(hJ : G.J = тИЕ)` hypothesis is added to `IsSigmaSeparated`: the LN treats the $J = \emptyset$ precondition as a *call-site* fact about the consumer's CDMG (handed in as an explicit hypothesis by e.g. `claim_3_22`), not a logical condition baked into the predicate.
